tactical_shape: central overload from Australia caused Paraguay problems
Julio Enciso carrying the attack on his own
Alexandro Maidana provided width from left-back/left side before half-time
standout_players: Orlando Gill — 6; crucial saves in opening and closing 10 minutes
Gustavo Gómez — 6; steady at the back, helped stifle Irankunda
Julio Enciso — 7; carried the team forward on his own and caused problems in the second half
fatigue_injuries: Omar Alderete suffered a knee injury just after the hour and never really recovered
Alexandro Maidana was pulled at half-time
home_read.momentum: A hard-fought clean sheet keeps Paraguay in the game, but the punditry suggests they were short of support around Enciso and lacked cutting edge.

tactical_shape: back three with wing-backs/full-backs, including Jordy Bos given licence to attack
midfield central overload and line-breaking runs from O’Neill and Irvine
comfortably handled Paraguay’s press at times and progressed the ball through midfield
standout_players: Patrick Beach — 6; calm and confident, including a stoppage-time save
Alessandro Circati — 7; tidy on the right of the back three and kept Enciso largely quiet
Harry Souttar — 7; anchored the back three with assurance and physicality
Jordy Bos — 8; his most impressive game so far, with more licence to gallop forward
Aiden O’Neill — 7; more present, linked well with Irvine
Jackson Irvine — 7; midfield general, crucial in breaking out of Paraguay’s press
Cristian Volpato — 7; Australia’s best player in the first hour, lively on the right
fatigue_injuries: Jacob Italiano was injured and unavailable, with Jordy Bos starting in his place
Jackson Irvine picked up a silly yellow early in the second half
Connor Metcalfe was hit in the face after the first hydration break and lost momentum
away_read.momentum: Australia look organised and resilient, with their structure and midfield control earning a respectable result despite lacking a final breakthrough.
